SEARCH
Package basicFiles.files

Class Folder

java.lang.Object
basicFiles.files.BaseFile
basicFiles.files.Folder
public class Folder extends BaseFile
Class representing a directory capable of containing files and other folders. This implementation follows the Composite Pattern, allowing a folder to treat individual files and sub-folders uniformly as BaseFile objects.
Author:
Elias Kassas

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Folder(File folder, String fileName)
    Constructs a folder within a specific parent directory.
    Folder(String folderName)
    Constructs a folder in the current working directory.

    Adds a file or sub-folder to this folder.

  • Constructor Details

    • Folder

      public Folder(File folder, String fileName)
      Constructs a folder within a specific parent directory. Automatically creates the physical directory on disk if it does not exist.
      Parameters:
      folder - The parent directory.
      fileName - The name of the folder.

    • Folder

      public Folder(String folderName)
      Constructs a folder in the current working directory.
      Parameters:
      folderName - The name of the folder.

  • Method Details

    • add

      public void add(BaseFile component)
      Adds a file or sub-folder to this folder. Marks the newly added component as out-of-sync to ensure it is written during the next save.
      Parameters:
      component - The BaseFile component to add.

    • remove

      public void remove(BaseFile component)
      Removes a file or sub-folder from this folder's internal registry.
      Parameters:
      component - The BaseFile component to remove.

    • read

      public void read() throws IOException, ClassNotFoundException
      Reads the contents of the physical directory and populates the children map.
      Specified by:
      read in class BaseFile
      Throws:
      ClassNotFoundException - Currently thrown as this method is not yet implemented.
      IOException - If a disk access error occurs.

    • write

      public void write(Object... args) throws Exception
      Recursively writes all unsynced children to the disk. This method uses a read lock to safely iterate through the children and ensures that only modified or new elements are physically written.
      Specified by:
      write in class BaseFile
      Parameters:
      args - Optional parameters passed down to the children's write methods.
      Throws:
      Exception - If an error occurs during the writing of any child element.

    • deletePhysicalFile

      public boolean deletePhysicalFile()
      Recursively deletes all physical files and sub-directories contained within this folder, followed by the folder itself.
      Specified by:
      deletePhysicalFile in class BaseFile
      Returns:
      true if the folder and all its contents were successfully deleted; false otherwise.

    • getChildren

      public List<BaseFile> getChildren()
      Retrieves a list of all child components contained within this folder.
      Returns:
      A List of BaseFile objects.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class BaseFile

    • append

      public void append(Object... args) throws Exception
      Appending is not supported for folder structures.
      Specified by:
      append in class BaseFile
      Parameters:
      args - Ignored.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - Always thrown as this operation is invalid for folders.
      Exception - If an error occurs during the append operation.
